item.title: This Mediterranean-style diet could keep your brain sharp as you age
item.description: The food choices we make over decades — not just in later life, but across adulthood — may quietly shape the health of our brains in ways that only become visible much later.

item.title: Queensland rising in battle against WA and SA for next AFL heartland
item.description: The question of who loves footy the most has sparked countless hours of debate over the past century, and Queensland is staking a major claim.

item.title: Family launches palliative care foundation after 33-year-old son's final hospital experience
item.description: Ryan Bowman fought through congenital heart defects and cancer, but his family says his final hospital visit led to some frightening final hours.

item.title: What we know and don't know about the Iran war negotiations
item.description: US President Donald Trump's surprising claim that talks with Iran were yielding great progress only raised more confusion over a war whose goals were already unclear.

item.title: The Gold Coast has transformed over 50 years. Its growth debate hasn't
item.description: Fifty years after fears it would be a "concrete jungle" first surfaced, the Gold Coast is again grappling with the same question: how to grow without losing its identity.

item.title: Seasons clash as a polar blast joins summer cyclone on Australia's weather map
item.description: A clash of seasons is developing on Australia's weather map this week as a tropical cyclone tracks towards the WA west coast and a polar blast with thunderstorms forms over the south-east.

item.title: Our interest in electric vehicles has grown due to oil price spikes. And it's likely to remain
item.description: The clear signal this trend data sends is that Australians are a pragmatic lot. If using an EV might save them money, then they are interested.

item.title: South Australia seek successive Sheffield Shield titles in final against Victoria
item.description: Victory in this year's Sheffield Shield final over Victoria will be especially sweet for South Australia, with coach Ryan Harris saying their path to the title decider this time around has been challenging.

item.title: Low-pressure system forecast to rapidly form in Tasman Sea
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Stephen Colbert to co‑write new Lord of the Rings film after late-night show ends
item.description: Talk show host Stephen Colbert, famous devotee to JRR Tolkien's Middle Earth, is co-writing a Lord of the Rings movie with his son.

item.title: WA coast 'pummelled' as Cyclone Narelle could reach category five on approach
item.description: Authorities in Western Australia say they are preparing for weather not seen since 2021, when a deadly cyclone flattened buildings in a town unaccustomed to such extreme conditions.

item.title: Sea the 'greatest risk and biggest asset' for beachside residents on Victoria's Bellarine and Barwon coasts
item.description: For residents of Victoria's Bellarine Peninsula like Vicki, who is facing the impacts of climate change-induced erosion and inundation, the water near their doors is both their biggest asset and greatest risk.

item.title: Law reform review calls for overhaul of WA's 'strictest' guardianship secrecy laws
item.description: People living under a guardianship arrangement in WA can't publicly speak up, but a review says this has to change.

item.title: PM calls second emergency national cabinet meeting over fuel crisis
item.description: States and territory leaders will convene next week to further coordinate the national response to the fuel crisis as the fallout from the Iran war continues to escalate.

item.title: Sheffield Shield final: Alex Carey unbeaten as South Australia's top order rocked by Victoria before rain
item.description: Victoria sent South Australia in to bat on day one of the Sheffield Shield final and claimed three wickets before rain stopped play.

item.title: Controversial Melbourne nightclub Love Machine destroyed by fire
item.description: Victoria Police are investigating a suspicious fire that tore through a controversial Melbourne nightclub.

item.title: Hannah Gadsby, Rove McManus and Luke Heggie recommend comedians to see at the comedy festival
item.description: Comedy season is in full swing, with festivals across the country hosting the best acts from Australia and the world. We asked our favourites to share their recommendations.

item.title: TGA proposes reforms to sunscreen regulation including new testing technologies
item.description: The proposals include a number of changes to SPF testing requirements, oversight of the labs that do SPF testing, and the way SPF is labelled — reforms that would overhaul the sunscreen industry.

item.title: Leigh Ryswyk says reaction to being first openly gay AFL player 'overwhelming'
item.description: Former Brisbane Lions player Leigh Ryswyk says the support he received after becoming the first AFL player to come out as gay has "blown me away".

item.title: Deklin Donnelly fronts court over alleged abduction and murder of Chris Baghsarian
item.description: The man who police have alleged to be a "major player" in the mistaken identity kidnapping and murder of a Sydney grandfather, Deklin Donnelly, has fronted court for the first time.

item.title: Federal government urges rise in minimum wage above rate of inflation
item.description: In its submission to the Fair Work Commission's annual wage review, the government again calls for the lowest-paid workers to not go backwards.

item.title: AFL concussion class action expands to more than 100 former players and 11 clubs
item.description: Eight more footballers have joined a multimillion-dollar concussion class action against the AFL, tipping the total to over 100 former players who all claim they have been left brain-damaged by the game.

item.title: After a lopsided and uncertain start to the season, the NRL risks losing itself in a maze of set restarts
item.description: After three rounds of the NRL season the increase in set restarts has left coaches, players and fans alike more confused than ever about the specifics behind rugby league's most controversial rule change.

item.title: AFL reveals new illicit drug policy that Andrew Dillon says is 'most stringent in Australian sport'
item.description: The AFL says its new illicit drugs policy, which includes biannual hair-testing of all players, is the most stringent in Australian sport.

item.title: SA premier says regional areas will be prioritised for fuel deliveries amid global oil crisis
item.description: South Australia's premier has assured farmers battling with soaring diesel prices that regional areas will be first to receive fuel deliveries.

item.title: AI detecting crocodiles in Queensland waterways
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: AI crocodile detection trials begin in north Queensland
item.description: Researchers are trialling artificial intelligence to detect crocodiles in north Queensland waterways, but its success depends on human training to ensure accuracy.

item.title: Federal government's ban on Iranian tourists to block 7,000 visa holders from Australia
item.description: Thousands of Iranian travellers have now been barred from entering Australia under a government ban, prompting criticism by refugee advocates and some crossbenchers.

item.title: Amateur lobster fisher escapes attack by 3.5m great white shark off Cape Jaffa
item.description: A fisher known as "Lucky Luke" to his mates lives up to his nickname after surviving a great white shark dragging him along the ocean floor while lobster fishing off the South Australian coast.

item.title: States to seek standardised reporting of fuel supply levels at national cabinet meeting
item.description: State and territory leaders are also expected to push for a national approach to any further measures, including fuel rationing, if supply were to run short.

item.title: Bill to change emergency fuel laws and enable price cap passes Tasmanian Parliament
item.description: A bill to give the Tasmanian government power to cap fuel prices has passed the state's parliament after a late-night sitting.

item.title: Queensland man charged with foreign incursion offence over alleged plans to fight in Myanmar civil war
item.description: Police allege Attilio Ivor Filip Dallavalle travelled to Thailand in January with plans to travel onward to Myanmar to engage in "hostile activities" with an armed group engaged in the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Sydney private school teacher accused by police of grooming 14-year-old girl
item.description: A casual teacher at Newington College has been charged with allegedly encouraging a 14-year-old girl to send sexually explicit images for money.

item.title: Centre for Disease Control issues health warning after outbreak of respiratory diphtheria in NT
item.description: The Northern Territory's health department has declared an outbreak of respiratory diphtheria in the Top End.

item.title: Socceroos approaching last chance saloon, with World Cup dreams in the balance
item.description: The Socceroos' upcoming games against Cameroon and Curaçao could make or shatter World Cup dreams, and Socceroos legend Mark Schwarzer has backed the players, and coach Tony Popovic, to deliver come tournament time.

item.title: Risk of vegetable price rise in response to spiralling fuel and fertilizer costs
item.description: A snap survey by AUSVEG reveals 27 per cent of Australian vegetable growers have already reduced planting, while some will not harvest current crops because rising costs make it unviable.

item.title: Mount Alexander shire urges government to force fossil fuel companies to fund climate disaster recovery
item.description: A Victorian council still cleaning up after a bushfire that wiped out more than 50 homes and businesses wants fossil fuel companies to pay for climate disaster recovery.

item.title: Evacuations in Dubbo's CBD after crane collapses in powerful storm
item.description: The crane was buffeted by a powerful storm before the boom came loose and fell onto a nearby apartment building.

item.title: Funding for Macquarie Point precinct inches forward after key plans approved by federal government
item.description: Federal funding for Hobart's waterfront redevelopment and stadium has reached a key milestone, with the Australian government accepting the site's master and housing plans.

item.title: Superintendent Sachin Sharma sues NT Police for demotion 'because of race'
item.description: A former NT Police Acting Assistant Commissioner has claimed his career is in tatters after he was overlooked for promotion and demoted for "reasons including" his race.

item.title: Fuel dominates politics, Hanson confused over Rinehart flight questions
item.description: The ongoing fuel crisis dominated politics this week but a probe into the National Anti-Corruption Commission was also announced and Pauline Hanson was grilled about accepting free flights from Gina Rinehart.

item.title: Why One Nation is not winning many lower house seats in the SA election despite its high vote
item.description: One Nation has received more than 20 per cent of the primary vote in South Australia, and so far, its lower house seat count remains slight. Why is that, and why are onlookers so interested in the result?

item.title: Sidon is facing a new wave of displaced residents from Lebanon's south as Israel's offensive continues
item.description: Southern Lebanon has a "very long history" of conflict with Israel, and many of its residents remember past wars clearly. But they say the latest incursion feels different.

item.title: Last-touch non-call labelled 'farce' as Geelong edges Adelaide in thriller
item.description: Geelong continues its streak over Adelaide at Kardinia Park, as one of the AFL's off-season rule changes is put squarely in the spotlight for the wrong reasons.

item.title: Sixteen-year-old girl the fifth person charged over death of Aidan Becker at Mernda railway station
item.description: A 16-year-old girl is the fifth person to be charged following the death of a 22-year-old man at a train station in Melbourne's outer north earlier this month.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Cherry-Evans has last laugh as Sea Eagles slump to third-straight defeat
item.description: Daly Cherry-Evans is roundly booed on his return to Brookvale after his off-season switch from the Sea Eagles to the Roosters, as the tricolours dominate territory to claim 33-16 victory.

item.title: At 104, George Franklin is months away from becoming the world's oldest conductor
item.description: WA musician George Franklin has already achieved a milestone by turning 104. He could soon garner another: the record for being the world's oldest band conductor.

item.title: Rising fuel prices drive surge in demand for emergency support in Canberra
item.description: Rising petrol and diesel prices are driving Canberra families to seek emergency assistance and forcing small businesses to consider whether to pass on higher costs to customers.

item.title: Qantas ramps up services to Europe as travellers avoid Middle East stopovers
item.description: Qantas will increase capacity on flights between Australia and Europe after Easter, which experts say may see the "kangaroo route" evolve.

item.title: Injured man, 54, survives three days in rural NSW creek after vehicle rollover
item.description: The 54-year-old remarkably survived three days injured in a rural NSW creek before a local farmer spotted him.

item.title: Severe storm causes thousands of power outages and hundreds calls for help in Sydney
item.description: Fire and Rescue NSW says there has been more than 400 calls for help, including one incident where a tree fell on a house trapping the occupant.

item.title: International Olympic Committee bans transgender athletes from women's events
item.description: Under changes announced by the International Olympic Committee, the gender of athletes will be determined by a one-time gene-screening test.

item.title: Former Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro appears in New York court after January capture
item.description: Former Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro and his wife return to the New York courtroom after being captured by US forces in January.

item.title: Eight-fold increase in EPA odour complaints in Melbourne's west linked to small number of businesses
item.description: Adam Pattison spent two years doing up his backyard to enjoy outdoor barbecues, but says "sickening" smells kill the vibe five times a week.

item.title: Red alert in Carnarvon as Cyclone Narelle closes in — as it happened
item.description: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting the WA coast.

item.title: Review of Tasmania's climate laws finds net zero status could be 'temporary'
item.description: Tasmania loves to boast about its net zero emissions. But a new report has found that status could be "temporary" if further actions are not taken.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: Jet fuel shortages, headbutting whales and Donald Trump's social faux pas
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Medical drama Grey's Anatomy marks 21 years of love, loss and disasters
item.description: Twenty-one years ago today, a group of interns walked into the fictional Seattle Grace Hospital to start their surgical careers. It was the start of the longest-running medical drama in US TV history.

item.title: Fuel price spike drives multi-million-dollar blowouts on public works
item.description: Industry groups warn some infrastructure projects could stall without additional government support to cover fuel-related costs.

item.title: Oil crisis revives talks of levy on plastic packaging to wean Australia off imports
item.description: The war in Iran renews the government's interest in reducing Australia's dependence on imported packaging, particularly oil-reliant plastic.

item.title: Albanese government struggles under 'stress test' posed by Middle East war
item.description: Fuel prices are the immediate issue for the Albanese government but it will also be under scrutiny for how it handles Australia's relationship with the United States and its volatile president.

item.title: Superannuation admin provider Grow Inc's financial trouble raises concerns for HESTA
item.description: Experts are concerned one of Australia's largest super funds has invested in a new admin company whose most recent financial report indicates it is in financial difficulty.

item.title: How 'traditional' gender roles are making a comeback in modern society
item.description: It turns out generation Z has some of the strongest traditional beliefs about gender roles. The revival of what many see as outdated gender norms is being attributed to "manosphere" content on social media.

item.title: Vanished: Six months of searching for Gus Lamont
item.description: Six months to the day since four-year-old Gus Lamont was reported missing from a vast outback station, many more questions than answers remain.

item.title: Advocates call for funding of transplant strategy to arrest declining organ donation rate
item.description: Vanessa Hujber did not think twice before donating her kidney to save the life of her daughter, Amelia. But Amelia was one of the lucky ones, with advocates calling for urgent action to arrest Australia's declining living organ donation rate.

item.title: Police mark 40th anniversary of Melbourne's Russell Street bombing
item.description: The 1986 car bomb attack on Victoria Police headquarters in Melbourne claimed the life of 21-year-old constable Angela Taylor and injured more than 20 others.

item.title: Male victim-survivors of childhood sexual abuse overrepresented in suicide statistics, but seldom spoken about
item.description: Craig Hughes-Cashmore spent more than 20 years living with persistent suicidal thoughts, an experience that many share but rarely feel able to discuss.

item.title: Second alleged 'crew' charged over Bankstown woman Thi Kim Tran's kidnapping, murder
item.description: Further charges have been laid over the alleged kidnapping and murder of a western Sydney woman who police believe was targeted due to her husband's organised crime links.

item.title: Daly Cherry-Evans's return to Brookvale Oval a look into the past as Manly faces uncertain future
item.description: With Manly's season teetering on the edge, the Sea Eagles faithful welcomed former skipper Daly Cherry-Evans to Brookvale Oval in enemy colours for the first time. But it was the prodigal son who won the day.

item.title: Almost one in three eligible voters in Coober Pedy were unable to cast a ballot
item.description: Nearly one in three Coober Pedy voters, or more than 250 people, missed their chance to vote in the state election last week after the town was left without a polling booth on election day.

item.title: ASX shares in lacklustre territory as markets focus on Middle East war duration — as it happened
item.description: Australian shares finished broadly flat today. The Australian dollar managed to breach 69 US cents, but trading was lacklustre. For more on all of the ramifications, see how the day unfolded on our business blog.

item.title: Mudgee Medical Centre secures six doctors to reopen books after 18 months
item.description: A medical centre in Central West NSW is accepting new patients for the first time in 18 months, after community support helped attract six new doctors.

item.title: Max Verstappen kicks Guardian F1 reporter out of Japanese Grand Prix press conference
item.description: Max Verstappen refuses to speak to media until Guardian journalist Giles Richards leaves because of a question he asked last year.

item.title: Petition calls for release of police video of Indigenous man's death in custody
item.description: Raylene Nixon says the public should be able to make their own minds up about what happened to her son in his final moments.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle smashes west Pilbara coast, tearing off roofs in Exmouth
item.description: Residents on WA's north-west coast are feeling the full brunt of severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle as it barrels across the region, tearing off roofs and flooding streets, with the system now downgraded to a category three.

item.title: Wind power off Victoria's coast could reduce need for new transmission lines, report finds
item.description: Developing Victoria's wind power sector could save the state money and powerline infrastructure, while providing stability for Eastern Australia's power grid, a new report shows.

item.title: Boat master jailed over illegally caught sea cucumbers in Far North Queensland
item.description: Authorities found almost two tonnes of the lucrative sea cucumbers on board a foreign fishing boat that was hidden in mangroves near a remote river in the country's far north earlier this month.

item.title: Goulburn community solar farm plugs in after 12-year journey
item.description: Hundreds of residents of a rural NSW town combine to build their own commercial solar farm, demonstrating the community benefits of renewable energy.

item.title: Cape York stations hit by Cyclone Narelle call for army's help to clean up
item.description: Floodwaters may have receded around the Archer River but the clean-up is just beginning after Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle tore through last week.

item.title: Big Red Bash cancelled for 2026, outback tourism facing uncertain season as fuel prices bite
item.description: This festival usually sees people go all out, spending thousands of dollars and travelling thousands of kilometres, but a potential change in location and fuel concerns have deterred ticket holders.

item.title: How Australians interact with birds and wildlife around their homes
item.description: Our recent stories about attracting native fauna to gardens and feeding wild birds resonated strongly. Here is how some Australians interact with wildlife in their local areas.

item.title: 'QuitGPT' boycott could spark AI flashpoint as ethical use debate rages
item.description: AI companies are stuck in a "perfect storm" of trying to control the use and output of their platforms while maintaining competitive profits, as millions of people boycott the technology, experts say.

item.title: 'Surreal' feeling as snow falls in March across parts of New South Wales
item.description: It is still only March, but snow is falling in parts of NSW and the Bureau of Meteorology is checking its record books.

item.title: Agencies warn elderly could suffer as fuel price rises hit aged care services
item.description: The national peak body for aged care providers is calling on the federal government to provide urgent support to keep services running, as the Middle East war sends fuel prices skyrocketing.

item.title: More than 1,000 Australians physically assaulted every day, new ABS crime data shows
item.description: Almost 400,000 Australians experienced physical assault in 2024-2025, according to new victimisation data released by the Australian Bureau of Statistics.

item.title: Gladstone's Jake Lyle to star in Phantom of the Opera on Sydney Harbour
item.description: Off sick from school, it was "love at first sight" for Jake Lyle the first time he saw Phantom of the Opera. Now the central Queensland talent is playing his dream role as Phantom on Sydney Harbour.

item.title: Mould infection cluster report identifies RPA balcony as likely exposure site, one patient remains in ICU
item.description: A balcony at the Royal Prince Alfred Hospital has been identified as the infection site of a mould cluster linked to two patient deaths late last year.

item.title: PM leaves door open to halving fuel excise temporarily
item.description: The prime minister leaves the door open to halving taxes on petrol and diesel, after the Coalition demands temporary relief from sky-high fuel prices.

item.title: Conflict reshapes Persian New Year for Iranian Australians
item.description: As Iranian Australians try to celebrate Persian new year, news of the conflict in the Middle East is always front of mind.

item.title: Sheffield Shield final: Victoria's top order wobbles as South Australia misses bonus point chance
item.description: Victoria and South Australia meet in the final of the Sheffield Shield at Melbourne's Junction Oval.

item.title: Fuel surcharges across a broad range of industries are adding to consumers' financial stress
item.description: Fuel surcharges are being applied across a range of industries, from the delivery of bricks to building sites to Australia's biggest rideshare companies, meaning consumer prices are rising.

item.title: Can states cap fuel prices? Tasmanian emergency fuel laws explained
item.description: The Tasmanian government is seeking to have the power to cap petrol prices. But how will it work? And if it does, can other states do the same?

item.title: Driver sentenced to more than 13 years in jail over hit-and-run of Canberra schoolboys
item.description: It's almost a year to the day since Tayler Hazell drove a stolen car erratically through Canberra, seriously injuring two children.

item.title: Bodycam footage shows tactical police officers using force on a handcuffed hit-and-run driver
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Anti-logging protester Ruth Langford/Tipruthanna requests court hearing on Tasmanian Aboriginal land
item.description: A member of the Tasmanian Aboriginal community charged with trespass has applied to the Hobart Magistrates Court to have her case heard on Aboriginal-owned land.

item.title: As Pakistan positions itself as a US–Iran broker, it draws on a set of relationships few countries can replicate
item.description: Pakistan is positioning itself at the centre of a war it is uniquely placed to influence and cannot afford to ignore, writes Meghna Bali.

item.title: Liberals to retain SA opposition status despite One Nation's second seat, ABC projects
item.description: The ABC is projecting that the Liberals have retained the Adelaide Hills seat of Heysen, meaning the party will be the second largest in the House of Assembly and retain opposition status.

item.title: AFL admits Adelaide wronged by crucial umpire error late in Crows' defeat to Geelong
item.description: Geelong's Tom Atkins convinced umpires a free-kick was his in a decision the AFL has since clarified should have gone Adelaide's way in a tight contest.

item.title: New Spirit of Tasmania ferry to be Dark Mofo 2026 floating art gallery
item.description: The new spirit of Tasmania ferries might not be available for journeys across the Bass Strait yet — but this winter, one of them will take on a new role as an art gallery, during Hobart's Dark Mofo festival.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Narelle heads inland amid reports of widespread damage in Exmouth
item.description: Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle continues to cut a destructive swathe through Western Australia's Gascoyne even as it weakens, with authorities warning the extreme weather is not over yet.

item.title: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says budget will include tax reform, but no decision yet on capital gains
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers commits to tax reform in his upcoming budget, but adds that no decision has yet been taken as to whether capital gains tax will be part of those changes.

item.title: Agnes Water man charged with 3D printing firearm parts following police investigation
item.description: Police say the charges come amid a concerning rise in the use of 3D printing technology to manufacture weapons.

item.title: AFL players to lose insurance coverage for brain injuries
item.description: More than 500 AFL players will soon no longer be covered by insurance for brain injuries in their superannuation after Zurich Insurance moved to cut their ability to claim for concussion and head trauma.

item.title: Authorities urge NSW beachgoers to cancel plans amid hazardous weather, surf conditions
item.description: New South Wales beaches will be too dangerous to visit this weekend, with authorities issuing a warning to rock fishers, swimmers and boaters to stay away from the coastline.

item.title: Diesel fuel stolen after thieves break into rural property in central Tasmania
item.description: Several hundred litres of diesel fuel have been stolen during an overnight break-in on a rural property in central Tasmania.

item.title: Queensland opposition says government broke promise on adult crime adult time advice
item.description: A report from the expert legal panel advising the state government's adult crime, adult time policy has been released, explaining the framework for the advice, rather than everything provided to the government.

item.title: Kelpie with head wounds found tied to tree at Adelaide Hills golf course
item.description: A kelpie that was found with "horrific" head wounds at an Adelaide Hills golf course has prompted the RSPCA to issue a public appeal for information. Warning: This story contains details some readers may find distressing.

item.title: Broncos claim pressure-relieving victory over Dolphins, ending tough week on a high
item.description: The Broncos overpowered the Dolphins 26-22 in the Battle of Brisbane, after Wests Tigers stunned ladder leaders New Zealand Warriors in Auckland. Follow live.

item.title: Police search remote Far North Queensland property where human remains found
item.description: The remains were found in the yard after police were called to the property for a welfare check on a 70-year-old man. An identity has not yet been confirmed.

item.title: Fans rethink driving to SA for AFL Gather Round as global fuel crisis continues
item.description: The SA government and AFL are in discussions to try to keep fans flocking to Adelaide for next month's AFL Gather Round, as the global fuel crisis threatens to disrupt travel plans.

item.title: Magpies confirm 'hamstring awareness' for Moore after win over Giants
item.description: Collingwood is expecting to be without its skipper Darcy Moore and his predecessor Scott Pendlebury next week against Brisbane after the pair were hobbled during the win over GWS.

item.title: Proposed new laws allow police to move people on for 'causing anxiety' without committing a crime
item.description: Young people could be moved on by police officers for "causing anxiety" without ever committing a crime under proposed Queensland laws.

item.title: Friday Finance with Dan Ziffer
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Six-day Telstra outage in remote NT community of Kintore impacting Triple Zero calls
item.description: Residents in the remote NT community of Kintore have had difficulty making Triple Zero calls during a six-day Telstra outage, which has also caused issues buying fuel.

item.title: Manly sacks coach Anthony Seibold after three games of 2026 season
item.description: Manly has sacked head coach Anthony Seibold after a dismal start to his fourth NRL season at the helm.

item.title: Police called to Canberra Centre after reports of teenagers brandishing knives, hammer
item.description: Police say they were called to the Canberra Centre about 6:20pm on Friday after reports that a group of young people were involved in a disturbance, brandishing knives and a hammer.

item.title: Socceroos snatch late win against Cameroon as Jordy Bos delivers and teenager Lucas Herrington impresses
item.description: Jordy Bos hits the back of the net as the Socceroos leave it late against Cameroon in Sydney with 18-year-old Lucas Herrington impressing on debut.

item.title: Savannah Guthrie to return to Today show after mother's disappearance
item.description: Savannah Guthrie is scheduled to return to her job as a co-anchor of NBC's Today show on April 6, more than two months after her 84-year-old mother's unresolved abduction.

item.title: Man interviewed on suspicion of sex trafficking in Mohamed Al-Fayed investigation
item.description: Former Harrods boss Mohamed Al-Fayed, who died in 2023, has been accused of rape, sexual assault, sexual exploitation and human trafficking on a large scale.

item.title: Ukraine and Saudi Arabia sign 'mutually beneficial' air defence deal
item.description: The deal comes at a crucial time for Kyiv, as US Secretary of State Marco Rubio has said weapons for Ukraine could be diverted to the Iran war

item.title: Trump struggles to find an off-ramp as an oil crisis meets a munitions crisis
item.description: While markets struggle to price a sudden collapse in oil supply, military analysts are sounding the alarm on the rate countries are burning through weapons stockpiles.

item.title: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle still packing strong winds as system moves inland
item.description: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle weakens as it travels south through WA's Wheatbelt, leaving a trail of destruction in its wake.

item.title: From Robyn to Courtney Barnett — these are the best albums of March
item.description: The year might've kicked off with a flood of great records, but March is when things really snap into gear.

item.title: Best books out in March, from Francis Spufford, Asako Yuzuki, Laura McCluskey and more
item.description: Wondering what to read next? Gothic crime fiction, a cult Japanese bestseller and a fantastical twist on the Blitz are among our critics' latest recommendations.

item.title: Why the end of the Iran war could be just the start of Australia's involvement
item.description: The federal government is turning its mind to what Australian support in the Middle East might look like after a ceasefire.

item.title: Claims gas exporters on 'best behaviour' as government intervention looms
item.description: With war in the Middle East throwing energy markets into chaos, Australian gas prices are strikingly subdued. Experts say looming intervention by Canberra may explain why.

item.title: TGA head defends handling of Australia's sunscreen scandal over SPF test results
item.description: In his first interview since the sunscreen controversy erupted almost 10 months ago, the Therapeutic Goods Administration head says he hopes the proposed shake-up of regulations will restore consumer confidence.

item.title: Online travel site Guest Reservations accused of fleecing customers with hidden fees and charges
item.description: As holiday-makers gear up for Easter, online travel site Guest Reservations is accused of fleecing customers using hidden fees and charges with advocates calling on government and the regulator to step in.

item.title: Donald Trump wants a 'silver bullet' in Iran but he's dealing with a 'very complex situation'
item.description: Donald Trump is deploying thousands of marines and extra battleships to the Middle East, while also claiming to be close to a deal with Iran to end the war. So is he set to wind down his war with Iran, or is he thinking of escalating?

item.title: US 'on verge' of ending Iran war as allies warned to lead postwar security coalition in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tells G7 leaders to ready a postwar coalition to secure the Strait of Hormuz, saying the US-Israeli war on Iran could conclude within weeks.

item.title: This man went viral after surviving Bondi. Then the internet took a dark turn
item.description: Arsen Ostrovsky was on the ground, bleeding, at the Bondi attack when he decided to take a selfie. Within 2 hours it was circulating on the internet. In less than a day, it had become fodder for a global conspiracy theory.

item.title: The community behind Geelong music festival Jerkfest's unlikely success
item.description: As music festivals collapse, one Geelong kid's hobby has turned into a big day out.

item.title: Months out from Thriving Kids, workforce questions remain unanswered
item.description: Months away from the start of a huge shift in disability support for children, the workforce expected to deliver it has concerns about its readiness.

item.title: Dementia is Australia's top killer — yet most still miss the early signs
item.description: Dementia is the leading cause of death in Australia and stigmas around the disease can delay diagnosis. But some in the community are at work to break taboos.

item.title: Pakistan's conflict with Afghan Taliban intensifies in shadow of Middle East war
item.description: As global attention remains fixed on the sprawling war in the Middle East, another conflict is edging towards a dangerous threshold — one between nuclear-armed Pakistan and Taliban-controlled Afghanistan.

item.title: Growing pains as blueberry industry expands rapidly and appetites increase
item.description: Australians are eating and growing more blueberries than ever before, but questions are being asked about how production of the fruit is being regulated.

item.title: Live: Houthis join Iran war, Israel accuses Yemen of entering conflict
item.description: The Iranian-backed militant group took to X to make the announcement, saying it launched a salvo of ballistic rockets at military targets in southern Israel. It came after Israel’s military says it has detected a missile launched from Yemen toward its territory. Follow live.

item.title: NSW Waratahs snap losing streak with Super Rugby win over ACT Brumbies in Canberra
item.description: NSW Waratahs coach Dan McKellar heralds his side's much-needed 30-28 victory against bitter rivals the ACT Brumbies as a special memory for the club.

item.title: Katherine residents call for hospital relocation after flood evacuations
item.description: After Katherine's worst flood in 28 years, residents are calling for governments to act on a decades-old idea to relocate the NT town's hospital away from the flood-prone river.

item.title: David Quirk's Porepunkah comedy show explores tragic transformation of his hometown
item.description: David Quirk is adamant his new stand-up show, Porepunkah, won't make fun of last year's police shooting, but it wouldn't exist without it.

item.title: Reform or handout? That is the question for the Government ahead of the budget
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Treaty elections underway in Victoria as changes flow from nation-leading agreement
item.description: Victoria's treaty elections are underway, with successful candidates taking on new responsibilities. But uncertainty remains, with the opposition sticking to its pledge to scrap the agreement if elected in November.

item.title: Australian Iranians hit back over 'racist' decision to block 7,000 tourist visa holders
item.description: Thousands of Iranian travellers have been barred from entering Australia under a government ban targeting people who might overstay their visas, prompting criticism by refugee advocates.

item.title: Iran's soccer team honours victims of deadly airstrike on primary school
item.description: Iran soccer players hold small backpacks to honour the victims of a deadly missile strike on a girls' primary school, before a friendly against Nigeria.

item.title: Tiger Woods arrested following car crash near Florida home
item.description: Golf legend Tiger Woods is arrested in Florida after refusing to submit a urine sample following a car crash near his Florida home.

item.title: Hawksbill sea turtle returns to ocean after months-long recovery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Critically endangered hawksbill sea turtle returns to wild months after beach rescue
item.description: What passers-by thought was a "sandy rock" was in fact a critically endangered turtle far from home.

item.title: Measles on the rise across NSW, concern community infections spreading undetected
item.description: An outbreak of measles in south-east Asia has fuelled a surge in NSW cases — but the state's biggest cause for concern is five infections believed to have been spread undetected in the community.

item.title: Adelaide 36ers beat Sydney Kings to level NBL finals, Kendric Davis and Bryce Cotton clash
item.description: Sydney coach Brian Goorjian insists he's not concerned by Kendric Davis's post-match tirade at Adelaide superstar Bryce Cotton, following the 36ers' NBL championship-levelling 91-89 win over the Kings.

item.title: The strange death of Liberal England and the lessons for Liberal Adelaide
item.description: Last week's South Australian election has left the Liberals in the grip of what one analyst described as an "existential crisis". As the Liberals seek to learn the lessons of the past, they should bear in mind the example of their once mighty namesakes, who ruled Britain for decades before their party turned to dust.

item.title: Thousands remain without power as ex-Cyclone Narelle continues south — as it happened
item.description: The storm, which has already made landfall in Far North Queensland and the Northern Territory, is now impacting Western Australia.

item.title: Running influencers are making big money and inspiring many to get active — but there are risks
item.description: Recently, online discussion has turned to what some perceive to be a spate of influencers getting injured.

item.title: Military action lights up skies in Jerusalem, Israel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: End of an era for Tharwa General Store after a century being owned by one family
item.description: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

item.title: Critical bridges across Lebanon's Litani River targeted in calculated invasion plan
item.description: Israel's expanded invasion of southern Lebanon was at least 12 days in the making, with bridges targeted to effectively cut off the south from the country's centre.

item.title: Israel Defense Forces release footage of the bombing of the Dlafy Bridge
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Roger Tuivasa-Sheck signs for English club Wakefield Trinity, to leave Warriors at end of NRL season
item.description: Just hours after the Warriors were beaten for the first time this NRL season, the Kiwi club have suffered another surprise setback.

item.title: Toowoomba Royal Show in full swing despite soaring fuel costs
item.description: Showmen are determined to keep putting on agricultural shows for regional Australia despite the "frightening" cost of fuel.

item.title: Isolated school students face difficult decisions amid surging fuel prices
item.description: Ruben Driver has a round trip of 4,000 kilometres to reach his boarding school. The cost of fuel is placing his family under immense pressure to get him to class.

item.title: Victoria in the box seat against South Australia in Sheffield Shied final with first-innings points
item.description: Victoria is closing on the Sheffield Shield title after a dramatic day's play against South Australia, punctuated by confusion around an injury substitution.

item.title: EPA, developer defend approach amid mounting criticism over Smiths Beach green light
item.description: The City of Busselton says the watchdog has ignored its own rules after giving the Smiths Beach development the green light, but Fini Group says its work was "rigorous".

item.title: Manly Sea Eagles premiership winner Kieran Foran named interim coach
item.description: Club legend Kieran Foran is appointed Manly's interim coach, after the Sea Eagles sacked Anthony Seibold just three games into the 2026 NRL season.

item.title: Timelapse of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle's path
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wall Street drops again for a fifth straight week as Iran war uncertainty lingers
item.description: US stocks have deepened their drops as Wall Street finished off a fifth straight week, its longest such streak in nearly four years.

item.title: Newly married couple buy Hervey Range heritage-listed former hotel in North Queensland
item.description: A 160-year-old hotel sat on the market for four years, until Jason and Kyla Romaior decided to take a risk. Insurance costs are $20,000 a year, but they are confident they have what it takes to handle the challenges of owning a heritage building.

item.title: Ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle disrupts WA's domestic gas supply, port operations
item.description: Gas giants Woodside, Santos and Chevron confirm they have suffered production outages at its gas as a result of ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle.

item.title: Government to underwrite fuel, fertiliser shipments
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How an Irukandji jellyfish the size of a fingernail causes 'unimaginable pain'
item.description: A tiny jellyfish found in Queensland has venom so powerful it causes feelings of "impending doom".

item.title: 'Not acceptable': Xerri facing ban after rubbing blood on Dons skipper's face
item.description: North Melbourne survives a nervy finish to break a decade-long drought against Essendon as star ruckman Tristan Xerri rubs blood on Andrew McGrath's face in a heated exchange.

item.title: Albanese’s new fuel powers ‘sensible thing to do’: Tony Wood
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Trump's new tourism envoy once proposed banishing pigeons from Sydney streets
item.description: US President Donald Trump has named Nick Adams as his new special presidential envoy for tourism. It isn't the Australian's first time in government.

item.title: Huge swells, hazardous surf batter NSW south coast beaches causing damage and erosion
item.description: Huge swells have inundated NSW beaches as most of the coastline remains closed to the public due to hazardous surf conditions.

item.title: Rylan Wooster's record-breaking run.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Meet the 11-year-old Qld schoolboy who just broke two national run records
item.description: A rising Queensland runner is drawing national attention after dominating three events — but said his dream sport lies elsewhere.

item.title: Not-for-profit steps in to bridge digital divide in remote Ngalingkadji Indigenous community
item.description: Residents from remote Ngalingkadji in Western Australia's north are being connected to the internet at home for the first time, bringing banking services, telehealth and emergency services.

item.title: Brown butter, oat and chocolate biscuits that are ready in no time - ABC News
item.description: Salty, sweet, chocolatey and buttery, these cookies are a Frankenstein melding of the best kind.

item.title: Two teenagers in custody, two on the run and paramedic injured after Melbourne crash
item.description: Police say four teenagers in a stolen car collided with an older couple in Carlton this morning, with two fleeing the scene immediately.

item.title: PM announces new powers to boost fuel supply amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The Albanese government will use public money to underwrite the delivery of extra cargoes of fuel in a bid to shore up supply during the Middle East conflict.

item.title: In one of the world's most visited countries, the local population is vanishing
item.description: Greece's population could drop by as much as 20 per cent by 2050, with the government declaring the situation a "national threat".

item.title: Surfers in Sydney amid damage on NSW coast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lifeguard assesses damage in Thirroul
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia women defeat West Indies by 103 runs in first ODI
item.description: Australia power to a 103-run win over the West Indies, despite Stafanie Taylor's breakthrough one-day century.

item.title: Cowboys score three tries in eight minutes to complete comeback against Storm
item.description: The Cowboys completed a second half comeback, scoring three tries in eight minutes, to defeat the Storm 28-24.

item.title: Services Australia urges patients to check if they are owed some of $272 million from Medicare
item.description: Services Australia is urging people to check if they're owed money from a $272 million pool of unclaimed money.

item.title: Bank of America agrees to pay $105 million to settle Epstein-related lawsuit
item.description: The Bank of America has agreed to pay $US72.5 million ($105.3 million) to settle a civil lawsuit brought by women who accused the bank of facilitating their sexual abuse by Jeffrey Epstein, court records show.

item.title: Palestinian Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah speaks to sell-out sessions at Newcastle Writers' Festival
item.description: Her inclusion at the event was slammed by NSW Premier Chris Minns, but Dr Abdel-Fattah told an audience in Newcastle today that art and literature can be spaces for political expression.

item.title: Lambs sold for $500 each as national flock dwindles towards record low
item.description: Australian sheep and lamb prices keep rising despite global uncertainty. But how long will consumers be willing to pay?

item.title: Brisbane Broncos player Josiah Karapani banned from Fortitude Valley precinct after alleged 'disturbance'
item.description: Police issued Mr Karapani with a public nuisance infringement and banning notice early on Saturday morning, after an alleged "disturbance".

item.title: Three deaths overnight after spate of road crashes across Queensland
item.description: Queensland has recorded three new traffic fatalities over the past 24 hours. There were 64 fatalities recorded in the state from January 1 to March 22, compared to 49 in the same period last year. 2025 marked a 16-year high for road deaths.

item.title: Tristan Gourlay breaks Australian record for solo, unassisted circumnavigation of the world
item.description: Tristan Gourlay has broken the Australian record for the fastest solo, unassisted circumnavigation of the world — completing it in 138 days. The record he broke was set by his father, Ken Gourlay, in 2007.

item.title: A dust storm stirred by up Cyclone Narelle moved through Shark Bay
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wind and rain from Cyclone Narelle sweeps through Exmouth's marina.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle isolates Exmouth, destroys airport and cuts essential services
item.description: The northern WA tourist town of Exmouth has been left isolated and heavily damaged after Cyclone Narelle tore through the town.

item.title: Yemeni protesters rally in solidarity with Iran and Lebanon
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Baby dies after rideshare crash in Lynwood, two others seriously injured
item.description: A 7-month-old child has died after a rideshare vehicle crashed into a tree in Perth's south-east, leaving two other passengers seriously injured.

item.title: Half of women have been harrassed while running, new data shows, as NSW safety program gets $500,000 boost
item.description: Fifty per cent of female runners in NSW have experienced harassment, new research shows, with an overwhelming majority saying safety issues dictate their behaviour.

item.title: St John Ambulance NT reaches 'critical failure point' during 'extraordinary event' in Darwin
item.description: Darwin's ambulance service has reached a "critical failure point" as 42 per cent of Triple Zero calls were abandoned overnight.

item.title: A carport patio withstands the full force of Cyclone Narelle at its peak.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Oscar Piastri qualifies third at Japanese GP for McLaren as Kimi Antonelli claims pole
item.description: Australia's Oscar Piastri of McLaren qualifies third fastest for the Japanese Grand Prix, behind the Mercedes duo of Kimi Antonelli and George Russell.
